Bartlett pear tree producing in abundance! It is producing 50+lbs per week. Access: Added by owner.
Falling Fruit access: Added by owner.
2 healthy pecans trees. One at entrance to a parking lot and another behind parking lot by a basketball hoop. Squirrels very active tho! Falling Fruit season: November-December.
Blackberry tumble next to the sidewalk on an incline next to abandoned lot with torn down house. Falling Fruit season: April-July.
Younger, wide-spread trailing blackberry bramble patch on the north side of the railway track easement, on side of rocky slope. About 10-20 feet from 6th ave. Access: Public.
Figs overhang sidewalk, however the conifer next to it overshadows the ficus tree. Access: Private but overhanging.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Often not visible under other foliage and frequently attacked by the city, there are several fig plants under the billboard. Access: Private but overhanging.

Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.

There's a side trail of the Vulcan main trail, that takes you up close to the big radio antenna, near 19th terrace. It's may be a little difficult to find, but well worth it in the summer time (very bountiful, and delicious!) Access: Private.
Dewberries cover the bushes out in front of the auto dealer. They ripen mid-late spring (April, May) Falling Fruit season: April-June.
Bountiful Fig tree overhanging parking lot of the neighborhood market Access: Private but overhanging.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Very large pecan tree. Area looks like it may be slated for development. Access: Permitted by owner.
Falling Fruit access: Permitted by owner.
Falling Fruit record for American persimmon.
Persimmons ripen in fall. Be sure not to harvest too early in fall, otherwise they'll give you cottonmouth. Falling Fruit season: October-November.
Two fig trees in abandoned community garden. May or may not be producing yet. Worth looking at though. Access: Public.
